name: Scindapsus treubii 'Moonlight Variegated'
origin: Peninsula Malaysia, Borneo, and Java; variegated collector form in cultivation
aboutText: Scindapsus treubii 'Moonlight Variegated' is an exceptionally beautiful climbing vine native to Malaysia, Borneo, and Java. Prized for its thick, leathery leaves with a matte silver moonlight overlay, this cultivar features striking white-to-cream sectoral patches over the silver-green base. It climbs via aerial roots and grows in lowland forest canopies, requiring high humidity and filtered light to flourish.

morphology.variegation: White to cream patches or marbling over silver-green base
morphology.growthHabit: Climbing with aerial roots, compact upright when supported

fieldNotes.title: Silver Moon and Cream Splashes
fieldNotes.author: Ariod Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Scindapsus treubii 'Moonlight' is prized for its thick, leathery, silver-satin leaves that shimmer under soft lighting. The variegated form introduces striking white-to-cream sectoral patches over the silver-green base. Native to Malaysia, Borneo, and Java, this vine climbs via aerial roots and grows in lowland forest canopies. The silver overlay is a specialized cell layer that diffuses light, and the variegation creates a multi-layered, three-tone color scheme that is highly coveted by collectors.
